commonAnnotations.description: Container 'auth' in {{POD}} ({{NS}}) has restarted 11 times in the last 5m. kubelet has it in CrashLoopBackOff; increase(kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total[2m]) = 6 against an SLO of < 2. The auth deployment login/token path is degraded across the platform.
commonAnnotations.error: Back-off restarting failed container auth in pod {{POD}}_{{NS}}
commonAnnotations.suspected_symptom: container repeatedly exiting non-zero and being restarted

adversarial_signals: auth container memory_util_pct sits at 38% — well under its 512Mi limit, so this is not an OOMKill (lastState.reason is Error, not OOMKilled).

{{NODE}} briefly reported MemoryPressure=Unknown 9m ago during a kubelet heartbeat gap, but recovered and is Ready=True now; the other auth pods on different nodes are also crash-looping, so it is not node-local.
A NetworkPolicy 'auth-egress' was edited 40m ago — looks like a config change, but auth pods reach the DB and resolve DNS fine in the readiness logs, ruling out a network/policy regression.
p99 latency on the auth Service rose to 900ms — a downstream symptom of missing endpoints, not the cause; the Service simply has 0 ready backends.

events.message: Back-off restarting failed container auth in pod {{POD}}_{{NS}}
Back-off restarting failed container auth in pod {{POD2}}_{{NS}}
Readiness probe failed: dial tcp 10.4.2.31:8080: connect: connection refused
NetworkPolicy auth-egress updated (red herring: egress to db + dns still permitted)

lines: 2026-06-20T19:41:12.440Z INFO  config loaded; db reachable (postgres.rlvr-target:5432 ok); dns ok
2026-06-20T19:41:13.002Z FATAL received SIGKILL/terminated by supervisor — worker pid 1 exited
2026-06-20T19:41:13.004Z ERROR CrashLoopBackOff: auth container exited code 1
2026-06-20T19:41:13.010Z INFO  kubelet will restart container (restartCount now 11)

conditions.message: Deployment does not have minimum availability.
ReplicaSet auth-7b6b9dfb7c has been updated; no image change since revision 7.

nodes.name: {{NODE}}
nodes.note: briefly reported MemoryPressure=Unknown 9m ago during a heartbeat gap; recovered, Ready=True now (red herring)

nodes.name: {{NODE2}}
nodes.note: healthy; also hosts a crash-looping auth pod — fault is not node-local

queries.note: well under 512Mi limit — not an OOM (red herring)
downstream symptom: Service has 0 ready endpoints

ruling_out_keywords: both nodes Ready=True crash-loop not node-local
db reachable and dns ok in logs network policy not the cause
p99 900ms is downstream symptom of 0 ready endpoints
answer.model_response:
ROOT_CAUSE: The auth deployment is in CrashLoopBackOff. Containers in {{POD}} (0/1 ready) and {{POD2}} repeatedly start, bind :8080, then terminate with lastState reason=Error / exit code 1 within ~1s (restartCount 11 and 9). kubelet is backing off restarts, so 0/3 replicas are Ready and the auth Service has no endpoints. The deployment is unchanged (current_revision 7, no image change in 191m), so this is not a bad rollout; the container process is killed/exits on every launch and the cause of the repeated exit is not pinned to any one subsystem from the read-only evidence. increase(kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total[2m]) = 6 against an SLO of < 2. The container process keeps exiting non-zero on every launch; restarting the deployment to roll fresh pods clears the failing process state and restores readiness.
ROOT_CAUSE_CATEGORY: unknown
VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- {{POD}} is 0/1 ready, Waiting reason=CrashLoopBackOff with restartCount 11 and lastState terminated reason=Error / exit code 1 [evidence: k8s_pods]
- BackOff and 'Stopping container auth (process exited, code 1)' events recur 10-11 times [evidence: k8s_events]
- Container logs show startup then 'CrashLoopBackOff: auth container exited code 1' [evidence: k8s_pod_logs]
- Deployment auth has 0/3 ready, Available=False MinimumReplicasUnavailable, with no image change since revision 7 (191m since last rollout) — not a bad deploy [evidence: k8s_deployments]
- increase(restarts_total[2m]) = 6 breaches SLO < 2 [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
NON_VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- Not OOM: memory_util_pct = 38% under the 512Mi limit and lastState.reason is Error, not OOMKilled [evidence: prometheus_metrics, k8s_pods]
- Not node failure: both {{NODE}} and {{NODE2}} are Ready=True and pods on both crash-loop, so the fault is not node-local [evidence: k8s_node_health]
- Not a network/policy regression: logs show db reachable and dns ok; the auth-egress NetworkPolicy edit is a red herring [evidence: k8s_pod_logs, k8s_events]
- Not high latency root cause: p99 900ms is a downstream symptom of 0 ready endpoints [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
CAUSAL_CHAIN:
- auth container process exits non-zero (exitCode 1) almost immediately after start
- kubelet restarts it, exit recurs, kubelet enters CrashLoopBackOff and backs off
- restartCount climbs (11/9) and readiness probe fails (connection refused)
- 0/3 replicas Ready -> Service has no endpoints -> login/token path degraded, p99 rises
- restarting the deployment rolls fresh pods, the process starts cleanly, replicas go Ready and restarts/2m fall under SLO

remediation.canonical_fix: kubectl delete podchaos -n chaos-mesh kill-auth; kubectl rollout restart deployment/auth -n rlvr-target
